/home/zbb/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/de8d048e-cfd0-4afe-a7bb-f8a13d91e484.jsonl:47:{"type":"message","id":"7e0c5175","parentId":"b9f9b116","timestamp":"2026-03-26T01:34:15.722Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_EB4gcCMm3x5cCBNIuiZL7pnT","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"---\nname: polyclaw\ndescription: \"Trade on Polymarket via split + CLOB execution. Browse markets, track positions with P&L, discover hedges via LLM. Polygon/Web3.\"\nmetadata: {\"openclaw\":{\"emoji\":\"🦞\",\"homepage\":\"https://polymarket.com\",\"primaryEnv\":\"POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY\",\"requires\":{\"bins\":[\"uv\"],\"env\":[\"CHAINSTACK_NODE\",\"POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY\"]},\"install\":[{\"id\":\"uv-brew\",\"kind\":\"brew\",\"formula\":\"uv\",\"bins\":[\"uv\"],\"label\":\"Install uv (brew)\"}]},\"clawdbot\":{\"emoji\":\"🦞\",\"homepage\":\"https://polymarket.com\",\"primaryEnv\":\"POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY\",\"requires\":{\"bins\":[\"uv\"],\"env\":[\"CHAINSTACK_NODE\",\"POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY\"]},\"install\":[{\"id\":\"uv-brew\",\"kind\":\"brew\",\"formula\":\"uv\",\"bins\":[\"uv\"],\"label\":\"Install uv (brew)\"}]}}\n---\n\n# PolyClaw\n\nTrading-enabled Polymarket skill for OpenClaw. Browse markets, manage wallets, execute trades, and track positions.\n\n## Features\n\n- **Market Browsing** - Search and browse Polymarket prediction markets\n- **Wallet Management** - Env-var based wallet configuration\n- **Trading** - Buy YES/NO positions via split + CLOB execution\n- **Position Tracking** - Track entry prices, current prices, and P&L\n- **Hedge Discovery** - LLM-powered covering portfolio discovery via logical implications\n\n## Quick Start\n\nFirst, install dependencies (from skill directory):\n\n```bash\ncd {baseDir}\nuv sync\n```\n\n### First-Time Setup (Required for Trading)\n\nBefore your first trade, set Polymarket contract approvals (one-time, costs ~0.01 POL in gas):\n\n```bash\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py wallet approve\n```\n\nThis submits 6 approval transactions to Polygon. You only need to do this once per wallet.\n\n### Browse Markets\n\n```bash\n# Trending markets by volume\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py markets trending\n\n# Search markets\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py markets search \"election\"\n\n# Market details (returns full JSON with all fields)\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py market <market_id>\n```\n\n**Output options:**\n- Default output is a formatted table (good for display)\n- Use `--full` flag for full question text without truncation\n- Use `--json` flag via `scripts/markets.py --json trending` for structured JSON output\n\n### Wallet Management\n\n```bash\n# Check wallet status (address, balances)\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py wallet status\n\n# Set contract approvals (one-time)\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py wallet approve\n```\n\nThe wallet is configured via the `POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY` environment variable.\n\n### Trading\n\n```bash\n# Buy YES position for $50\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py buy <market_id> YES 50\n\n# Buy NO position for $25\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py buy <market_id> NO 25\n```\n\n### Positions\n\n```bash\n# List all positions with P&L\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py positions\n```\n\n### Hedge Discovery\n\nFind covering portfolios - pairs of market positions that hedge each other via contrapositive logic.\n\n```bash\n# Scan trending markets for hedges\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py hedge scan\n\n# Scan markets matching a query\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py hedge scan --query \"election\"\n\n# Analyze specific markets for hedging relationship\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py hedge analyze <market_id_1> <market_id_2>\n```\n\n**Output options:**\n- Default output is a formatted table showing Tier, Coverage, Cost, Target, and Cover\n- Use `--json` flag for structured JSON output\n- Use `--min-coverage 0.90` to filter by minimum coverage (default 0.85)\n- Use `--tier 1` to filter by tier (1=best, default 2)\n\n**Coverage tiers:**\n- **Tier 1 (HIGH):** >=95% coverage - near-arbitrage opportunities\n- **Tier 2 (GOOD):** 90-95% - strong hedges\n- **Tier 3 (MODERATE):** 85-90% - decent but noticeable risk\n- **Tier 4 (LOW):** <85% - speculative (filtered by default)\n\n**LLM model:** Uses `nvidia/nemotron-nano-9b-v2:free` via OpenRouter. Model selection matters — some models find spurious correlations while others (like DeepSeek R1) have output format issues. Override with `--model <model_id>` if needed.\n\n## Security\n\nFor the MVP, the private key is stored in an environment variable for simplicity and Claude Code compatibility.\n\n**Security Warning:** Keep only small amounts in this wallet. Withdraw regularly to a secure wallet.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Required | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `CHAINSTACK_NODE` | Yes (trading) | Polygon RPC URL |\n| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Yes (hedge) | OpenRouter API key for LLM hedge discovery |\n| `POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY` | Yes (trading) | EVM private key (hex, with or without 0x prefix) |\n| `HTTPS_PROXY` | Recommended | Rotating residential proxy for CLOB (e.g., IPRoyal) |\n| `CLOB_MAX_RETRIES` | No | Max CLOB retries with IP rotation (default: 5) |\n\n**Security Warning:** Keep only small amounts in this wallet. Withdraw regularly to a secure wallet. The private key in an env var is convenient for automation but less secure than encrypted storage.\n\n## Trading Flow\n\n1. **Split Position** - USDC.e is split into YES + NO tokens via CTF contract\n2. **Sell Unwanted** - The unwanted side is sold via CLOB order book\n3. **Result** - You hold the wanted position, recovered partial cost from selling unwanted\n\nExample: Buy YES at $0.70\n- Split $100 USDC.e → 100 YES + 100 NO tokens\n- Sell 100 NO tokens at ~$0.30 → recover ~$27 USDC.e\n- Net cost: ~$73 for 100 YES tokens (entry: $0.73)\n\n## Polymarket Contracts (Polygon Mainnet)\n\n- **USDC.e:** `0x2791Bca1f2de4661ED88A30C99A7a9449Aa84174`\n- **CTF (Conditional Tokens):** `0x4D97DCd97eC945f40cF65F87097ACe5EA0476045`\n- **CTF Exchange:** `0x4bFb41d5B3570DeFd03C39a9A4D8dE6Bd8B8982E`\n\n## Dependencies\n\nInstall with uv (from skill directory):\n```bash\ncd {baseDir}\nuv sync\n```\n\n## Limitations\n\n- Trading requires wallet approval setup (one-time)\n- CLOB sells may fail if liquidity is insufficient\n\n### CLOB Cloudflare Blocking\n\nPolymarket's CLOB API uses Cloudflare protection that blocks POST requests from many IPs, including datacenter IPs and some residential ISPs. This affects the \"sell unwanted tokens\" step.\n\n**Solution: Residential proxy with retry logic**\n\nThe recommended setup uses a rotating residential proxy (e.g., IPRoyal, BrightData). The CLOB client automatically retries with new IPs until one works:\n\n```bash\nexport HTTPS_PROXY=\"http://user:pass@geo.iproyal.com:12321\"\nexport CLOB_MAX_RETRIES=10  # Default is 5\n```\n\nWith this setup, CLOB orders typically succeed within 5-10 retries as the proxy rotates through IPs until finding an unblocked one.\n\n**Alternative workarounds:**\n1. **Use `--skip-sell`** — Keep both YES and NO tokens, sell manually on polymarket.com\n2. **No proxy** — Split still works; only CLOB sell is affected\n\nIf CLOB fails after all retries, your split still succeeded. The output tells you how many tokens to sell manually.\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"No wallet available\"\nSet the `POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY` environment variable:\n```bash\nexport POLYCLAW_PRIVATE_KEY=\"0x...\"\n```\n\n### \"Insufficient USDC.e\"\nCheck balance with `uv run python scripts/polyclaw.py wallet status`. You need USDC.e (bridged USDC) on Polygon.\n\n### \"CLOB order failed\"\nThe CLOB sell may fail due to:\n- Insufficient liquidity at the sell price\n- IP blocked by Cloudflare (try proxy)\n\nYour split still succeeded - you have the tokens, just couldn't sell unwanted side.\n\n### \"Approvals not set\"\nFirst trade requires contract approvals. Run:\n```bash\nuv run python scripts/polyclaw.py wallet approve\n```\n\n## License\n\nApache 2.0\n"}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1774488855715}}

content.thinking:
**Diagnosing session issues**

I need to generate findings about the session bug without needing more tools. It seems like the direct session created with the oc_ chat ID is likely faulty. The user asked for "B," which suggests they want to investigate this further. I should respond with a diagnosis and propose next actions. We found malformed session metadata, so I might recommend cleaning up stale sessions and updating the cron delivery to use message send instead. Let's present these troubleshooting options.

content.text:
[[reply_to_current]] 查到一个很像根因的脏点了。

### 现在基本确认的事实

**1）群 session 是正常的**
- key：`agent:main:feishu:group:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`
- deliveryContext 也是对的：
  - `channel: feishu`
  - `to: chat:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`

这条是标准的“发到飞书群”。

---

**2）系统里还混进了一条明显不正常的 direct session**
我直接从 session 存储里查到了这条：

- key：`agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`

问题在于：

- `chatType: direct`
- 但它的标识居然是 `oc_...`

这就不对。  
在飞书里：
- **群** 一般是 `oc_...`
- **私聊用户** 一般是 `ou_...`

也就是说，系统里出现了一个：
> **把群 ID 当成私聊对象的 direct session**

这就是我前面说的那个很怪的点，现在实锤了。

---

### 这说明什么

大概率不是：
- 群 ID 配错
- 飞书机器人没权限
- 你看漏了

而是：

## **OpenClaw 内部会话路由污染了**
同一个 `oc_...` 群，被同时注册成了：

- 正常的 `group:oc_...`
- 异常的 `direct:oc_...`

这会导致 cron announce 这类“内部自动投递”在某些时候**命中了错误的会话路由**。  
于是系统内部觉得“我送到了”，所以记 `delivered=true`，但实际上并没有通过正确的飞书群通道落地。

这也能解释两件怪事：

1. **群里没消息，但 cron 说 delivered**
2. **13:30 那次还发出了“等一下 ticker。”这种过程句**
   
因为一旦走歪到错误 session，最终的结果抓取和投递链路都可能变得不稳定。

---

### 我现在的判断

根因优先级大概是：

**最高概率：**
- 历史上某次投递/路由，把 `oc_...` 错建成了 `direct` session
- 后续 cron announce 在 session 解析时被这个脏 session 干扰

**低一些的可能：**
- 飞书 channel 插件在 announce 投递时，对 `chat:oc_...` 的会话归类有 bug
- 或 cron delivery 解析 `to` 时没有强制按 `group/chat` 走，导致会话复用错了

---

### 下一步我建议怎么做

我建议分两步：

## 方案 1：先止血
以后这套 crypto 巡检不要完全依赖 cron 自带 announce 落地。

改成：
- cron 负责跑分析
- 真要发群时，用 **`message` 显式发到 `chat:oc_...`**

因为这个链路我们已经验证过是正常的。

---

## 方案 2：清掉脏路由
把这条异常 session：
- `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`

从会话存储里清掉/重置，避免它继续污染路由。

这个动作我建议谨慎做，但它是最像“修根因”的一步。

---

### 我的建议
先做这个顺序最稳：

1. **先把 crypto cron 的投递改成更稳的方式**
2. **再处理这条异常 direct session**

如果你点头，我下一步就去做两件事：
- 找出这几个 crypto cron 的具体 delivery 配置
- 定位怎么安全清理这个异常 `direct:oc_...` session

一句话版：

**查到的核心异常就是：系统里存在一条“把群 ID 当私聊 ID”的脏会话，这很可能就是 cron 显示 delivered、但群里没消息的根因。**

content.text:
[[reply_to_current]] 修的话，我建议分成 **“先止血”** 和 **“修根因”** 两步。

## 先止血：保证以后消息真能进群
最稳的临时方案是：

1. **保留现在的巡检 cron**
2. 但把“发到龙虾交易室”这一步，改成走**明确的群消息链路**
3. 也就是以后不要再信那条有问题的自动 announce 路由

原因很简单：  
我们已经验证过：

- cron 的 `delivered=true` **不可信**
- `message -> chat:oc_3f...` **是能正常发进群的**

所以短期要的是“别再丢消息”。

---

## 修根因：清掉那条错误 session
现在最像根因的是这条脏会话：

- `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`

它明显不正常，因为：

- `direct` 本该配 `ou_...`
- 现在却挂了一个群 ID `oc_...`

### 修复动作
大概就是这 4 步：

1. **备份当前 session 索引**
   - `/home/zbb/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`

2. **删除这条异常映射**
   - `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_3f35394f236997ca78b509c9fb71a383`

3. **视情况处理对应 session 文件**
   - 对应 sessionId 是：`a29dc134-d51b-4665-9ff8-863b33285c78`
   - 先不要硬删，建议先移走或改名备份

4. **重启 gateway**
   - 让会话路由重新加载

然后再做一次验证：

- 手动触发一条 crypto cron
- 看它是不是能正常落到 **龙虾交易室**
- 同时确认不会再生成新的 `direct:oc_...` 脏条目

---

## 风险点
这个修法风险不算大，但也不是零风险：

- 会影响这条异常 session 的历史映射
- 如果 OpenClaw 内部还有别的地方持续生成这条脏 session，删完可能还会再长出来
- 所以删完以后必须复测

---

## 我建议的实际执行顺序
最稳的是：

### 方案 A：保守修法
1. 先备份
2. 删掉异常 `direct:oc_...` 映射
3. 重启 gateway
4. 手动跑一次 crypto cron 验证
5. 如果还复发，再继续查插件/路由逻辑

这个我建议先做。

### 方案 B：只绕过，不碰根因
不删 session，只是以后都改成手动/显式群发。  
优点是稳。缺点是问题还在，系统会继续脏着。

---

## 我的判断
**应该先修。**  
因为现在已经不只是“偶尔丢消息”，而是系统里确实存在一条错误会话映射。这个不清掉，后面还会继续出怪事。

如果你同意，我下一步就直接帮你做这套：

- 备份 `sessions.json`
- 清掉异常 `direct:oc_...` 映射
- 备份对应 session 文件
- 重启 gateway
- 触发一次测试验证

你回我一句 **“修”**，我就开始动手。

content.text:
[[reply_to_current]] 可以限定，但要分两层说。

### 先说结论
**正常情况下，能限定 group。**  
像这种群消息，内部应该只走：

- `agent:main:feishu:group:oc_...`

而不该走：

- `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_...`

---

### 但你这次这个 `direct` 不像“你在 web 对话”那么简单
如果真是 web 私聊，对象通常应该是：

- `ou_...` 用户 ID

而你这条脏 session 是：

- `direct:oc_3f...`

也就是：
**它把一个群 ID（oc_）当成了私聊对象。**

这不是正常的“web 对话分流”，而是**路由归类错了**。

---

### 能不能强制只走 group？
**可以，思路上有三种强度：**

#### 1）调用层强制指定群目标
最直接的是每次发消息都显式写：

- `channel=feishu`
- `target=chat:oc_3f...`

这种最稳。  
我刚刚手动补发成功，就是走的这条。

---

#### 2）会话层只保留 group 映射
也就是把这种异常的：

- `direct:oc_...`

清掉，只保留：

- `group:oc_...`

这就是我刚才已经做的修复动作。

但问题是：  
**如果系统别的地方还会重新生成 `direct:oc_...`，它还是会复发。**

---

#### 3）配置/代码层限制“oc_ 只能当群”
这是最根治的方式。

逻辑上应该加一条判断：

- 如果目标是 `oc_...`
  - 强制归类为 `group`
  - 禁止创建 `direct` session

或者更严格点：

- `ou_...` 只能 direct
- `oc_...` 只能 group

这才是从源头堵住。

---

### 所以你问“能限定 group 吗”
答案是：

**能，而且应该。**  
但现在系统里不是“没法限定”，而是**某条链路没有守住这个限定**，导致 `oc_...` 被错误建成了 direct session。

---

### 我建议你现在这么理解
- **你的 web 对话**：会有 direct session，这正常
- **龙虾交易室这种群**：应该只存在 group session
- **这次异常**：不是“web 对话自然产生的 direct”，而是“群 ID 被错误归成 direct”

---

### 后面怎么做最稳
我建议分两层：

1. **业务层先兜底**
   - 群投递一律显式用 `chat:oc_...`
   - 不信任模糊路由

2. **系统层再修根因**
   - 加规则：`oc_` 不允许创建 direct session

如果你要，我下一步可以继续帮你做两件事里的一个：

- **A. 查现有 cron / delivery 有没有办法直接固定成 group-only**
- **B. 继续查 OpenClaw / Feishu 路由代码或配置，看哪里把 `oc_` 建成了 direct**

你想快点止血就选 **A**，想挖根因就选 **B**。

content.text:
[[reply_to_current]] 明白，那结果就很明确了：

**刚才那次修复只清掉了脏 session，但还没修到根。**  
因为如果根因只是在那条 `direct:oc_...`，清掉以后这次 18:45 的测试本该正常进群；但你现在确认**群里还是只有我手动补发的那条**，说明：

### 现在可以基本下判断
1. **手动 `message -> chat:oc_...` 是正常的**
2. **cron announce -> 群** 这条链路还是坏的
3. 问题不只是 session 脏映射，更像是：
   - cron 的 delivery/announce 对 Feishu 群路由有 bug
   - 或者 cron 完成后“自动投递到群”的实现没真正走 `message` 那条可靠链路

---

### 这意味着什么
短期别再赌 cron 自己会把消息送进群了。  
**目前唯一被验证可靠的群投递方式，就是显式用 `message` 发到 `chat:oc_...`。**

---

### 现在最稳的修法
我建议直接改策略，不再让 crypto 巡检依赖 cron 的 announce：

#### 稳妥方案
- cron 继续跑
- 但巡检结果不要靠 cron 自动投群
- 改成：
  - 要么发到当前会话/某个稳定 session
  - 要么由我/另一个固定流程再用 `message` 显式发到龙虾交易室

---

### 现在的排障结论
可以写成一句话：

**不是“分析没跑”，也不是“群配错了”，而是 `cron -> announce -> Feishu group` 这条链路本身不可靠；`message -> chat:oc_...` 才是可靠链路。**

---

### 我建议下一步直接做这个
我去把这几条 crypto cron **从“announce 到群”改成更稳的投递方案**。  
比如：

- cron 只负责运行
- 输出交给一个稳定会话
- 真要发群时显式用 `message`

这样就不会再出现：
- 系统说 delivered
- 群里却没消息

如果你同意，我下一步就直接去查这几个 cron 的具体配置，然后给你一个**可落地的改法**。

content.text:
---
name: web-search-pro
description: |
  Multi-engine web search with full parameter control. Supports Tavily, Exa, Serper, and SerpAPI
  with domain filtering, date ranges, deep search, news mode, and content extraction.
  Auto-selects the best engine based on query type and available API keys.
  多引擎精细化搜索：支持域名过滤、日期范围、深度搜索、新闻模式、内容提取。
  根据查询类型和可用 API Key 自动选择最优引擎。
homepage: https://github.com/Zjianru/web-search-pro
metadata:
  openclaw:
    emoji: "🔎"
    always: true
    requires:
      bins: ["node"]
      env: ["TAVILY_API_KEY"]
    primaryEnv: "TAVILY_API_KEY"
---

# Web Search Pro

Multi-engine web search with full parameter control for AI agents.
A precision supplement to OpenClaw's built-in `web_search` (Brave/Perplexity), providing
domain filtering, deep search, news mode, date ranges, and content extraction
that the built-in search does not support.

多引擎精细化网络搜索，为 AI Agent 设计。
作为 OpenClaw 内置 `web_search`（Brave/Perplexity）的精细化补充，提供域名过滤、
深度搜索、新闻模式、日期范围、内容提取等内置搜索不支持的能力。
配置一个或多个 API Key，自动选择最优引擎。

## Engines / 引擎

| Engine | Strengths | Free Tier | API Key Env |
|--------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| **Tavily** | AI-optimized, best answer quality, full filters, extract | 1000/month | `TAVILY_API_KEY` |
| **Exa** | Semantic/neural search, deep research | $10 credit | `EXA_API_KEY` |
| **Serper** | Real Google SERP, best news coverage | 100/month | `SERPER_API_KEY` |
| **SerpAPI** | Multi-engine (Google/Bing/Baidu/Yandex/DuckDuckGo) | 250/month | `SERPAPI_API_KEY` |

## Auto-Select Priority / 自动选择优先级

When `--engine` is not specified, the skill picks the best available engine:

| Query Type | Priority | Reason |
|------------|----------|--------|
| Default | Tavily > Exa > Serper > SerpAPI | Tavily has best AI answer + full filters |
| `--deep` | Tavily > Exa | Both have dedicated deep search modes |
| `--news` | Serper > Tavily | Google News has widest coverage |
| `--news --days` | Tavily only | `--days` is Tavily news-specific parameter |
| `--include-domains` | Tavily > Exa > Serper > SerpAPI | Tavily/Exa have native domain filters |
| `--search-engine baidu` | SerpAPI | Only SerpAPI supports Baidu/Yandex |

## Search / 搜索

```bash
# Basic search (auto-select engine)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query"

# Force specific engine
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --engine tavily

# Domain filtering (only search specific sites)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --include-domains "github.com,stackoverflow.com"

# Exclude domains
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --exclude-domains "pinterest.com,quora.com"

# Date range (absolute)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --from 2026-01-01 --to 2026-02-09

# Time range (relative)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --time-range week

# Deep/advanced search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --deep

# News search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --news --days 7

# Multi-engine: Baidu search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --engine serpapi --search-engine baidu

# More results
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" -n 10

# JSON output (for programmatic use)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --json
```

## Extract / 内容提取

Extract readable content from URLs (Tavily Extract or Exa livecrawl):

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "https://example.com/article"
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url1" "url2" "url3"
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url" --engine exa
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url" --json
```

## All Options / 全部参数

| Option | Description | Engines |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--engine <name>` | Force engine: tavily\|exa\|serper\|serpapi | all |
| `-n <count>` | Number of results (default: 5) | all |
| `--deep` | Deep/advanced search mode | tavily, exa |
| `--news` | News search mode | tavily, serper, serpapi |
| `--days <n>` | Limit news to last N days | tavily |
| `--include-domains <d,...>` | Only search these domains | all (native: tavily, exa) |
| `--exclude-domains <d,...>` | Exclude these domains | all (native: tavily, exa) |
| `--time-range <range>` | day\|week\|month\|year | all |
| `--from <YYYY-MM-DD>` | Results after this date | all |
| `--to <YYYY-MM-DD>` | Results before this date | all |
| `--search-engine <name>` | SerpAPI sub-engine: google\|bing\|baidu\|yandex\|duckduckgo | serpapi |
| `--country <code>` | Country code (us, cn, de...) | serper, serpapi |
| `--lang <code>` | Language code (en, zh, de...) | serper, serpapi |
| `--json` | Raw JSON output | all |

## Setup / 配置

Add API keys to your environment (e.g., `~/.openclaw/.env`):

```bash
# Configure at least one (recommended: Tavily)
TAVILY_API_KEY=tvly-xxxxx        # https://tavily.com (1000 free/month)
EXA_API_KEY=exa-xxxxx            # https://exa.ai ($10 free credit)
SERPER_API_KEY=xxxxx             # https://serper.dev (100 free/month)
SERPAPI_API_KEY=xxxxx            # https://serpapi.com (250 free/month)
```

## Notes / 说明

- At least one API key must be configured
- Metadata declares runtime prerequisites explicitly:
  - `requires.bins`: `node`
  - `requires.env`: `TAVILY_API_KEY` (primary onboarding key)
  - `primaryEnv`: `TAVILY_API_KEY`
- Other provider keys (`EXA_API_KEY`, `SERPER_API_KEY`, `SERPAPI_API_KEY`) are optional and used when `--engine`/auto-select routes to those engines
- `--search-engine <name>` always uses SerpAPI and requires `SERPAPI_API_KEY`
- `--deep` only works on Tavily/Exa; if both keys are missing, command exits with an error
- `--news` only works on Tavily/Serper/SerpAPI; if none are available, command exits with an error
- `--days` only works with Tavily news mode
- `--news --days <n>` auto-selects Tavily; if Tavily key is missing, command exits with an error
- Domain filtering via `--include-domains`/`--exclude-domains` works natively on Tavily and Exa; on Serper/SerpAPI it's implemented via `site:` query operators
- `--deep` mode uses more API credits (Tavily: 2x, Exa: varies)
- Extract only works with Tavily and Exa
- Missing/invalid option values fail fast with explicit CLI error messages (no silent fallback, no runtime TypeError)
- All output is Markdown-formatted for AI agent consumption; use `--json` for programmatic access
